Records.description: Two-loop massive kite graph (4-point) in $D=4$. Algorithm gives order 7 operator that factorizes into $L^{(1)} L^{(2)} L^{(3)} (z\, d/dz + 1)$, all factors with Liouvillian solutions only.

The kite graph $(2,1,2)$: 4 vertices, 5 edges, $L=2$, 4 external legs. Two trivalent vertices connected by two chains of length 2 and one single edge. Symanzik polynomials $U_{(2,1,2)}$ and $V_{(2,1,2);D}$ given explicitly. Proposition p:212: for $D\ge 2$ the quadric fibration on the blowup is non-degenerate; for $D=2$ the discriminant curve has geometric genus 0 so the cohomology is mixed Tate; for $D>2$ it has genus 1 and $\mathrm{Gr}^W H^3(X_{(2,1,2);4};\mathbb{Q}) = H^1(E)(-1)$ for an elliptic curve depending on mass and kinematic parameters. In $D=4$ the Feynman differential form lives in the complement of $U \cup F$, and the Picard-Fuchs operator agrees with the Weierstrass family after a scaling analysis, recovering the kite elliptic curve in Bloch 2021.
Records.method: graph hypersurface, quadric fibration, blowup, mixed Hodge structure, Picard-Fuchs, Griffiths-Dwork

Records.authors: Fevola, C., Mizera, S., Telen, S.
Records.description: Landau singularities of the acnode (triangle-box) computed via principal Landau determinants.
